items.title: UK: Energy scenarios shape BoE outlook – ING
items.summary: ING’s James Smith outlines scenarios for UK inflation, with current energy prices implying a brief 4% peak in autumn and ING’s base case of easing disruption pointing to a 3.5% peak in September.

items.title: Germany: War risks darken Ifo outlook – Commerzbank
items.summary: Commerzbank’s Chief Economist Dr. Jörg Krämer warns that the sharp March drop in the German Ifo Business Climate Index reflects rising war-related risks rather than current damage.

items.title: BoE's Greene: There is a risk inflation expectations will rise
items.summary: Bank of England Monetary Policy Committee member Megan Greene said on Wednesday that if their inflation forecasts are right, there is a risk inflation expectations will rise, per Reuters.

items.title: Iran sees truce and talks as not viable in current conditions – Fars
items.summary: Citing informed sources on the matter, Iran's Fars news agency reported on Wednesday that Tehran doesn't see truce and talks as viable in current conditions, despite the United States' increased efforts to establish a ceasefire and begin direct negotiations.

items.title: BoC: Dovish stance versus market pricing – TD Securities
items.summary: TD Securities strategist Andrew Kelvin notes that the Bank of Canada (BoC) is stressing downside risks to growth and appears willing to look through an energy-driven inflation shock.

items.title: Pakistan delivered US proposal to Tehran – Reuters
items.summary: Citing a source, who asked not to be named, Reuters reported on Wednesday that Pakistan has delivered the United States' proposal to Iran.

items.title: Euro Area: PMIs soften as inflation pressures rise – Deutsche Bank
items.summary: Deutsche Bank analysts report that Euro Area activity is slowing, with the composite PMI dropping to a 10‑month low while still just above the 50 expansion threshold. They emphasize that survey details point to rising input prices, suggesting renewed inflation pressures.

items.title: ECB’s Lane: Inflation readings could be higher in March and April
items.summary: European Central Bank (ECB) Chief Economist Philip Lane warned of higher inflationary pressures in the Eurozone in the next two months during European trading hours at the ECB and its Watchers conference at Goethe University in Frankfurt on Wednesday.

items.title: RBA: Energy shock keeps inflation risks elevated – UOB
items.summary: UOB Economist Lee Sue Ann expects the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) to keep the cash rate at 4.10% through 4Q26, after a split decision hike in March driven by Iran-related energy shocks and a strong labour market.

items.title: Lagarde speech: Economy may be more-quick to adjust if inflation rises
items.summary: European Central Bank (ECB) President Christine Lagarde signaled at the ECB and its Watchers conference at Goethe University in Frankfurt during European trading hours on Wednesday that the central bank is ready for monetary policy adjustments if inflation proves stronger.

items.title: Dow Jones futures rise on US-Iran peace proposal optimism
items.summary: Dow Jones futures inch higher 0.7% to near 46,750 during European hours, ahead of the United States (US) regular market open on Wednesday. Meanwhile, S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 futures gains 0.6% and 0.63% to near 6,650 and 24,360, respectively, at the time of writing.

items.title: UK: Persistent price pressures challenge BoE – Deutsche Bank
items.summary: Deutsche Bank’s Chief UK Economist Sanjay Raja notes that UK inflation data broadly matched expectations, with Headline CPI at 3% and stronger Services CPI driving a firmer Core CPI outcome.
